purpose: Defines the !profiles slash command — scans {tracker_root}/_commands/profiles/ for all .md files, extracts profile name (filename minus .md), role title (from ## Role heading or first line), and one-line summary, then renders a sorted table with Profile/Role/Best Used For columns. Includes usage reminder showing composition syntax: !{profile} {prompt}, !{profile} !{command} {prompt}, !{profile} !p {prompt}, !{profile} !p_track {prompt} (profiles compose with commands). Always scans live — never hardcode profile list. Reports 'No profiles found. Create one with: create a new profile called {name}' if directory empty/missing.
gotchas: Always scan _commands/profiles/ LIVE — never hardcode the profile list. New profiles must surface automatically.
Read EACH profile file to extract accurate role/usage info — do not guess from filenames alone.
Profiles compose with commands via !{profile} !{command} syntax — output reminder must include this.
Empty/missing directory: emit specific guidance message rather than silent failure.
conventions: Three-column table format: Profile / Role / Best Used For.
